question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a minimum node count requirement in the returned network map to inject a response with exactly one node pointing to an attacker-controlled server, causing all transactions to be gossiped exclusively to that server where they are dropped?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a nodesEnvelope where DomainName contains a URL with an embedded path (e.g. 'evil.com/path'), causing net.JoinHostPort to produce 'evil.com/path:port', an invalid address that the SDK cannot connect to, preventing gossip?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a response with HTTP 200 but an extremely slow body transfer (slow loris style), holding the HTTP connection open for the full cfg.mirrorNodeClientTimeout duration per attempt and across all retries, preventing the network map from being built and blocking all gossip?
